rows.title: European AI Office
rows.summary: Central EU institution for AI Act implementation and GPAI supervision, including codes, guidance, evaluations, and coordination.
rows.caveat: EU AI Office powers are tied to the AI Act; this institution row is not a separate national law.

rows.title: NIST Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI)
rows.summary: Technical AI evaluation and standards body with public frontier-model evaluation and procurement-evaluation links.
rows.caveat: CAISI rows describe standards/evaluation context, not an independent binding pre-release approval requirement.
rows.sourceName: NIST - Center for AI Standards and Innovation

rows.title: UK AI Security Institute
rows.summary: UK state-backed AI safety institute focused on advanced-AI research, evaluation, and global policy evidence.
rows.caveat: AISI testing and research are tracked as evidence/context unless a separate binding legal source applies.

rows.title: CEN-CENELEC Joint Technical Committee 21
rows.summary: European standardization body developing AI Act-supporting standards, including risk, quality, conformity, datasets, robustness, and cybersecurity topics.
rows.caveat: Standards and conformity-assessment rows are not national law unless a verified legal instrument incorporates them.

rows.title: U.S. Federal Trade Commission
rows.summary: U.S. consumer-protection and competition authority relevant to AI claims, deceptive AI products, and unfair AI-enabled conduct.
rows.caveat: FTC authority is tracked as existing-law enforcement context, not a comprehensive AI statute.

rows.title: Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat
rows.summary: Canadian federal public-sector algorithmic governance body behind the AIA tool and automated-decision risk assessment workflow.
rows.caveat: Public-sector policy context; it should not be read as a general private-sector AI law.

rows.title: Council of Europe Treaty Office
rows.summary: Official Treaty Office status source for the Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI.
rows.caveat: Treaty Office status is authoritative for treaty formalities; legal effect still depends on signature, ratification, entry into force, and domestic implementation.
rows.sourceName: Council of Europe Treaty Office - CETS No. 225

rows.title: European Data Protection Supervisor as AI Act supervisor for EU institutions
rows.jurisdiction: European Union institutions, bodies, offices, and agencies
rows.summary: EU AI Act supervisory hook for EU institutions and bodies, distinct from national AI Act enforcement.
rows.caveat: This row concerns EU institutions and bodies; it should not be read as a national competent-authority designation.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: Commissioner of Communications as Cyprus AI Act market-surveillance contact
rows.summary: Commission-listed Cyprus AI Act market-surveillance contact.
rows.caveat: Commission list confirms the contact; it does not fully describe domestic powers or sectoral enforcement arrangements.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: Minister for Enterprise, Tourism and Employment as Ireland AI Act market-surveillance contact
rows.summary: Commission-listed Irish AI Act market-surveillance contact.
rows.caveat: Commission list confirms the contact; it does not fully describe domestic powers or sectoral enforcement arrangements.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: National Cybersecurity Agency as Italy AI Act market-surveillance contact
rows.summary: Commission-listed Italian AI Act market-surveillance contact.
rows.caveat: Commission list confirms the contact; it does not fully describe domestic powers or sectoral enforcement arrangements.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: Consumer Rights Protection Centre as Latvia AI Act market-surveillance contact
rows.summary: Commission-listed Latvian AI Act market-surveillance contact.
rows.caveat: Commission list confirms the contact; it does not fully describe domestic powers or sectoral enforcement arrangements.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: Communications Regulatory Authority of the Republic of Lithuania as Lithuania AI Act market-surveillance contact
rows.summary: Commission-listed Lithuanian AI Act market-surveillance contact.
rows.caveat: Commission list confirms the contact; it does not fully describe domestic powers or sectoral enforcement arrangements.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: National Commission for Data Protection pending AI Act Single Point of Contact
rows.summary: Pending Commission-listed Luxembourg AI Act Single Point of Contact.
rows.caveat: Commission table marks this row with an asterisk, meaning final national adoption is still pending.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: Agency for Communication Networks and Services of the Republic of Slovenia pending AI Act Single Point of Contact
rows.summary: Pending Commission-listed Slovenian AI Act Single Point of Contact.
rows.caveat: Commission table marks this row with an asterisk, meaning final national adoption is still pending.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: Spanish Artificial Intelligence Surveillance Agency pending AI Act Single Point of Contact
rows.summary: Pending Commission-listed Spanish AI Act Single Point of Contact.
rows.caveat: Commission table marks this row with an asterisk, meaning final national adoption is still pending.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Market Surveillance Authorities under the AI Act

rows.title: Korea AI Safety Institute
rows.summary: Korean AI safety institute context for frontier-AI safety coordination and technical evaluation capacity.
rows.caveat: The official MSIT source verifies launch and network participation context; it does not by itself create lab-specific legal duties.
rows.sourceName: MSIT - Korea participates in International Network of AI Safety Institutes

rows.title: AI Verify Foundation
rows.summary: Singapore-linked AI assurance and testing foundation for trustworthy-AI tooling and evaluation ecosystem context.
rows.caveat: Issuer-controlled assurance body context; rows using it should not imply legal certification or binding state regulation.

rows.title: European Artificial Intelligence Board
rows.summary: EU AI Act coordination body linking member-state competent authorities, the AI Office, and implementation guidance work.
rows.caveat: Coordination and advisory context; this row is not a separate legal obligation or national regulator designation.

rows.title: AI Act Service Desk and Single Information Platform
rows.summary: EU AI Act implementation helpdesk and online platform for browsing the AI Act, checking compliance, and contacting expert support.
rows.caveat: Guidance and information hub; users should verify legal obligations against the official AI Act and adopted implementing materials.
rows.sourceName: AI Act Service Desk - Single Information Platform

rows.title: Canadian Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute
rows.summary: Canadian AI safety institute focused on advanced-AI risk science, synthetic-content risk, joint testing, and international coordination.
rows.caveat: AI safety research and coordination context; not a binding approval gate for frontier labs.
rows.sourceName: ISED - Canadian Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute

rows.title: Japan AI Safety Institute
rows.summary: Japanese technical AI safety institute centered on evaluation methods, standards, guidance support, and international cooperation.
rows.caveat: Technical safety and guidance-support context; it should not be read as a binding certification regime.

rows.title: Singapore AI Safety Institute
rows.summary: Singapore AI safety institute for frontier-AI testing, evaluation methods, policy research, and governance tooling.
rows.caveat: Safety research and testing context; it should not imply binding legal duties for all AI systems.

rows.title: EU draft guidelines on high-risk AI classification consultation
rows.summary: Open feedback process on draft guidelines for classifying high-risk AI systems under the AI Act.
rows.caveat: Draft guidance process; not a final legal interpretation and not itself a new binding rule.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - high-risk AI classification consultation

rows.title: EU draft guidelines on AI Act transparency obligations consultation
rows.summary: Targeted feedback process on draft guidelines for AI Act Article 50 transparency duties for interactive AI, generative AI, deepfakes, and related systems.
rows.caveat: Closed draft-guidance process; it should not be read as a final legal interpretation or a separate legal instrument.

rows.title: Ireland Regulation of Artificial Intelligence Bill pre-legislative scrutiny consultation
rows.summary: Irish parliamentary pre-legislative scrutiny consultation on the General Scheme of the Regulation of the Artificial Intelligence Bill 2026.
rows.caveat: Parliamentary process row; it should not be treated as enacted law or a final implementation model.
rows.sourceName: Houses of the Oireachtas - AI Bill 2026 public consultation

rows.title: UK Joint Committee on Human Rights inquiry on AI regulation
rows.summary: UK parliamentary inquiry examining how human rights can be protected in the age of AI and whether existing regulation is sufficient.
rows.caveat: Parliamentary inquiry process; it is evidence-gathering and policy scrutiny, not a binding legal instrument.
rows.sourceName: UK Parliament - Joint Committee on Human Rights AI inquiry

rows.title: European Parliament hearing on democracy and elections in the AI era
rows.summary: European Parliament joint hearing on how AI affects elections, disinformation, deepfakes, and democratic integrity.
rows.caveat: Parliamentary hearing context; it does not by itself create legal obligations.
rows.sourceName: European Parliament - Democracy and elections in the AI era hearing

rows.title: Council of Europe AI Framework Convention signature and ratification process
rows.summary: Ongoing signature and ratification process for the Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI.
rows.caveat: Treaty status depends on signature, ratification, entry into force, and domestic implementation; signature is not the same as ratification.
rows.sourceName: Council of Europe - Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence

rows.title: EU AI Office GPAI implementation tools and code-of-practice process
rows.summary: Ongoing EU AI Office process for AI Act GPAI implementation tools, codes of practice, guidance, and compliance-monitoring support.
rows.caveat: Process row; it should not be read as a separate obligation beyond the underlying AI Act and adopted implementing materials.

rows.title: CEN-CENELEC JTC 21 AI Act standardization work programme
rows.summary: European AI standards work programme supporting AI Act conformity, including risk management, quality management, conformity assessment, datasets, robustness, and cybersecurity.
rows.caveat: Standards and conformity-assessment rows are not national law unless a verified legal instrument incorporates them.

rows.title: UK AISI frontier AI trends and testing monitoring
rows.summary: AISI process for public evidence on frontier-model trends, capabilities, and testing.
rows.caveat: Monitoring and research process; not a certification or legal compliance determination.

rows.title: European Artificial Intelligence Board meeting and coordination process
rows.summary: Ongoing EU AI Act coordination process for national competent authorities, Commission guidance, and AI Board endorsed documents.
rows.caveat: Board meetings and endorsed documents are implementation and coordination context; they are not standalone binding duties unless adopted through the AI Act or related legal acts.

rows.title: CEN-CENELEC AI Act harmonized-standardization work
rows.summary: AI Act-supporting harmonized standards work covering risk management, transparency, human oversight, cybersecurity, quality assurance, and conformity assessment.
rows.caveat: Standards and conformity-assessment rows are not national law unless a verified legal instrument incorporates them.

rows.title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI management system standard
rows.summary: International AI management-system standard for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and improving AI governance processes.
rows.caveat: Standards and conformity-assessment rows are not national law unless a verified legal instrument incorporates them.

rows.title: NIST AI RMF standards and risk-management context
rows.summary: Voluntary AI risk-management framework used as technical governance context and referenced across public/private AI governance.
rows.caveat: NIST AI RMF is voluntary guidance unless a separate legal or contractual instrument makes it operative.

rows.title: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile context
rows.summary: Generative-AI profile for applying the NIST AI RMF to generative AI risks and controls.
rows.caveat: Voluntary U.S. technical guidance; not an international agreement or binding law.
rows.sourceName: NIST - AI RMF Generative AI Profile

rows.title: European Commission AI Act standardisation request
rows.summary: Commission request and work programme for CEN-CENELEC AI Act-supporting harmonized standards across risk, data, transparency, oversight, robustness, cybersecurity, quality, and conformity assessment.
rows.caveat: Standards and conformity-assessment rows are not national law unless a verified legal instrument incorporates them.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Standardisation of the AI Act

rows.title: prEN 18286 AI quality management system public enquiry
rows.summary: First AI Act harmonized-standard candidate to enter public enquiry, focused on quality management systems for EU AI Act regulatory purposes.
rows.caveat: Standards and conformity-assessment rows are not national law unless a verified legal instrument incorporates them.
rows.sourceName: European Commission - Standardisation of the AI Act

rows.title: EU AI Act notified-body list infrastructure
rows.summary: AI Act mechanism requiring the Commission to assign identification numbers and maintain a public list of notified bodies under the Regulation.
rows.caveat: This row tracks the legal/institutional mechanism, not a populated final list of AI Act notified bodies.

rows.title: EU AI Act Article 43 conformity-assessment routes
rows.summary: AI Act conformity-assessment context for high-risk AI systems, including internal-control and third-party notified-body routes depending on system category and harmonized-standard use.
rows.caveat: This is an explanatory conformity-assessment context row. The official AI Act text and any implementing or national designation acts control legal effect.

rows.title: NIST AI RMF Playbook
rows.summary: Voluntary NIST AI RMF companion playbook with suggested actions for Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions.
rows.caveat: Voluntary technical guidance; it becomes operative only through separate law, policy, contract, or organizational adoption.

rows.title: ISO/IEC 23894:2023 AI risk management guidance
rows.summary: Published ISO/IEC guidance standard on managing risk related to AI products, systems, and services.
rows.caveat: Issuer-controlled standards-body source; this is voluntary standards context unless incorporated by law or contract.

rows.title: ISO/IEC 42005:2025 AI system impact assessment guidance
rows.summary: Published ISO/IEC standard providing guidance for organizations conducting AI system impact assessments.
rows.caveat: Issuer-controlled standards-body source; this is voluntary standards context unless incorporated by law or contract.

rows.title: U.S. federal AI use case inventories
rows.summary: OMB-backed federal government AI use-case inventory reporting requirement and public inventory context.
rows.caveat: Public-sector transparency context; not a private-sector frontier-lab obligation.

rows.title: Canada Algorithmic Impact Assessment tool
rows.summary: Mandatory public-sector risk assessment tool for automated decision systems under Canadian federal digital-government policy.
rows.caveat: Applies to covered public-sector automated decision systems; not a general private-sector AI regulation.

rows.title: EU AI Act Article 71 high-risk AI systems database
rows.summary: AI Act database mechanism for high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III and related registration requirements.
rows.caveat: Tracks the legal database mechanism; it is not evidence that every required record is already publicly populated.

rows.title: Netherlands government Algorithm Register
rows.summary: Dutch public algorithm register focused on impactful algorithms, including high-risk AI systems.
rows.caveat: Public-sector transparency register; it should not be read as a full inventory of all AI systems or private-sector AI use.

rows.title: UK Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard hub
rows.summary: UK public-sector standard and repository for publishing information about algorithmic tools and algorithm-assisted decisions.
rows.caveat: Public-sector transparency obligation/context; it is not a private-sector frontier-lab law.
rows.sourceName: GOV.UK - Algorithmic Transparency Recording Standard Hub

rows.title: UK guidelines for AI procurement
rows.summary: Official UK guidance for public-sector buyers procuring AI technologies responsibly and evaluating AI suppliers.
rows.caveat: Procurement guidance and public-sector acquisition context; it is not a general private-sector AI law.

rows.title: Algorithm Charter for Aotearoa New Zealand
rows.summary: Public-sector algorithm transparency and accountability charter covering higher-risk algorithms and commitments on transparency, partnership, human oversight, privacy, ethics, and human rights.
rows.caveat: The charter covers public-sector algorithmic decision-making more broadly than AI and should not be read as a private-sector AI law.

rows.title: U.S. OMB M-25-21 federal AI use-case inventory requirements
rows.summary: Federal AI governance memorandum requiring agencies to continue annual AI use-case inventory reporting and publication of publicly releasable uses.
rows.caveat: Federal public-sector policy; it does not impose private-sector AI duties.

rows.title: U.S. Department of Justice 2025 AI use-case inventory
rows.summary: Agency-specific 2025 AI use-case inventory with DOJ AI uses across development, pilot, deployed, and retired stages.
rows.caveat: Agency inventory context; release limitations mean it should not be read as a complete operational audit of all DOJ AI use.
rows.sourceName: U.S. Department of Justice - AI Inventory

rows.title: U.S. OMB M-25-22 AI acquisition guidance
rows.summary: Federal procurement guidance for improving agency acquisition of AI capabilities in government.
rows.caveat: Public-sector acquisition guidance; it is procurement context and should not be treated as a general private-sector AI law.

rows.title: FTC Operation AI Comply
rows.summary: FTC enforcement sweep against deceptive AI claims and schemes.
rows.caveat: Enforcement context only; no tracked frontier lab is treated as a target without an official source.

rows.title: FTC Rytr AI review-generator proposed order
rows.status: FTC administrative complaint and proposed order
rows.summary: FTC Operation AI Comply matter alleging that Rytr's AI writing assistant enabled false and deceptive consumer reviews.
rows.caveat: Consumer-protection enforcement context; it is not a frontier-lab matter and does not create a new AI-specific statute.

rows.title: FTC Rite Aid facial-recognition order
rows.status: federal court case and FTC stipulated order
rows.summary: FTC case involving Rite Aid's use of facial recognition technology and safeguards for automated biometric systems.
rows.caveat: Existing-law enforcement context for biometric AI; no frontier lab is treated as a party or target.
rows.sourceName: FTC - Rite Aid Corporation, FTC v.

rows.title: SEC AI-washing settlements with investment advisers
rows.summary: SEC settled charges against investment advisers for allegedly false and misleading AI-use statements.
rows.caveat: Securities-enforcement context for AI claims; it does not establish a general AI-specific statute.

rows.title: FTC DoNotPay AI-lawyer deceptive-claims order
rows.summary: FTC case against DoNotPay over claims that its AI service could substitute for a human lawyer.
rows.caveat: Consumer-protection enforcement context; not a general finding about all legal-AI products or frontier labs.

rows.title: Dutch DPA Clearview AI facial-recognition fine
rows.summary: Dutch Data Protection Authority decision concerning Clearview AI's facial-recognition data collection.
rows.caveat: Official page can reject automated retrieval; keep as medium confidence until manually spot-checked in the next source review.
rows.sourceName: Dutch Data Protection Authority - Clearview AI fine decision

rows.title: Korean PIPC DeepSeek status examination
rows.summary: Korean PIPC status-examination results and recommendations concerning DeepSeek's R1 LLM chatbot service in Korea.
rows.caveat: Privacy regulator examination and recommendations; legal effect should be read from the official PIPC release and underlying PIPA, not generalized to all frontier labs.

rows.title: FTC In the Matter of Everalbum, Inc.
rows.summary: FTC alleged Everalbum deceived users about facial-recognition use and retained content after deactivation; resolved by consent order (Matter No. 192 3172).
rows.caveat: Consumer-protection / biometric enforcement context; no frontier lab is a party.
rows.sourceName: FTC - Everalbum, Inc., In the Matter of

rows.title: EEOC v. iTutorGroup, Inc.
rows.summary: EEOC's first AI-hiring-discrimination suit: iTutorGroup's tutor-application software allegedly auto-rejected applicants by age; settled for $365,000.
rows.caveat: Employment-discrimination enforcement; outcome is a settlement, not an adjudicated liability finding.

rows.title: FTC 6(b) inquiry into generative-AI investments and partnerships
rows.summary: FTC issued compulsory 6(b) orders to Alphabet, Amazon, Anthropic, Microsoft, and OpenAI to study generative-AI investments/partnerships. A study, not an allegation of wrongdoing.
rows.caveat: Formal regulatory study, not an enforcement complaint or finding of any law violation.
rows.sourceName: FTC - Inquiry into Generative AI Investments and Partnerships

rows.title: Thomson Reuters v. ROSS Intelligence Inc.
rows.summary: Landmark AI-training-data copyright case: ROSS trained a legal-research tool on data derived from Westlaw headnotes. In Feb 2025 the court largely sided with Thomson Reuters on infringement and fair use.
rows.caveat: Litigation status changes; this is a pre-trial summary-judgment posture as of 2025-02-11, not a final judgment.
rows.sourceName: GovInfo - Thomson Reuters v. ROSS memorandum opinion

rows.title: Mobley v. Workday, Inc.
rows.status: Partial denial of motion to dismiss (Second Amended Complaint)
rows.summary: Closely-watched case on whether an AI hiring-tools vendor can face disparate-impact liability; substantial age-discrimination claims survived dismissal.
rows.caveat: Litigation status changes; this reflects a motion-to-dismiss posture, not a merits ruling.

rows.title: The New York Times Co. v. Microsoft Corp. & OpenAI
rows.status: Centralized in MDL 3143 (In re: OpenAI, Inc. Copyright Infringement Litigation)
rows.summary: The flagship news-copyright case over training and outputs of OpenAI GPT models (and Microsoft products using them), now coordinated within MDL No. 3143. In April 2025 the court allowed core claims to proceed.
rows.caveat: The 2025-04-04 SDNY opinion is primary-verified; the later MDL milestones are from a research pass and should be confirmed against the MDL docket before being relied on.
rows.sourceName: SDNY - NYT v. Microsoft/OpenAI MTD opinion

rows.title: Andersen v. Stability AI Ltd.
rows.status: Ongoing pretrial litigation; plaintiffs moved for leave to file a Third Amended Complaint (2026-01-12)
rows.summary: Artists' class action alleging Stable Diffusion and related AI image tools (Stability AI, Midjourney, DeviantArt, Runway) were trained on their works and infringe copyright and right of publicity.
rows.caveat: Held for human review: the cited GovInfo PDF was not confirmed to be the specific February 2024 order, and the 2026/2027 hearing dates are from a research pass. Do not rely on procedural detail until a PACER/RECAP docket-authenticated order is obtained.
rows.sourceName: GovInfo - Andersen v. Stability AI order

rows.title: Getty Images v. Stability AI Ltd
rows.status: Interlocutory High Court judgment in ongoing IP litigation
rows.summary: UK copyright/database-right/trademark claim that Stability scraped Getty images to train Stable Diffusion and that outputs can infringe and bear Getty marks.
rows.caveat: Litigation status changes; interlocutory posture as of 2025-01-14, not a final judgment.
rows.sourceName: Courts and Tribunals Judiciary - Getty v Stability AI

rows.title: ICO investigation into Snap 'My AI'
rows.status: Preliminary Enforcement Notice; final decision not to enforce
rows.summary: ICO probed whether Snap adequately assessed data-protection risks (including to children) before launching the 'My AI' chatbot; concluded without an enforcement notice after Snap revised its DPIA.
rows.caveat: Privacy-regulator investigation that concluded without enforcement; not a finding of unlawfulness.

rows.title: ICO enforcement against Clearview AI Inc.
rows.status: Monetary penalty + enforcement notice (2022); UT remittal to FtT; Court of Appeal permission granted
rows.summary: ICO fined Clearview AI £7.55m and ordered it to delete UK data over scraping facial images into a searchable database. After tribunal proceedings, the Upper Tribunal restored the ICO's jurisdiction and remitted the appeal; Clearview has since been granted Court of Appeal permission.
rows.caveat: Do not treat the 2022 penalty as either finally enforceable or finally quashed: the verified status is remittal after the UT restored ICO jurisdiction, with a Court of Appeal challenge also pending (the 2025-12-19 permission is from a research pass).
rows.sourceName: ICO / Upper Tribunal - Clearview AI appeal judgment [2025] UKUT 319 (AAC)

rows.title: Garante provisional limitation on ChatGPT (OpenAI)
rows.jurisdiction: Italy (Garante per la protezione dei dati personali)
rows.status: Emergency regulatory action followed by ongoing inquiry
rows.summary: First major EU regulator action against ChatGPT: the Italian Garante ordered a provisional processing limit in March 2023 over privacy and age-verification concerns, later lifted after OpenAI measures.
rows.caveat: Investigation-stage event; paired with the later 2024 sanction record. Context only, not AI law.

rows.title: Garante sanction against OpenAI (ChatGPT), Decision No. 755/2024
rows.status: Agency sanction; annulled by Tribunale di Roma on competence grounds; decision removed from publication
rows.summary: The Italian Garante fined OpenAI €15m over ChatGPT data processing (Decision 755/2024); in 2026 the Court of Rome annulled the penalty on one-stop-shop competence grounds (without reaching the merits) and the decision was removed from publication.
rows.caveat: The annulment was on competence (one-stop-shop) grounds, not a merits clearance; do not state 'pending appeal' as fact — Cassazione status is unverified.
rows.sourceName: Garante - decision page (Decision 755 removed after Rome judgment)

rows.title: Joint investigation of OpenAI (PIPEDA Findings #2026-002)
rows.status: Complaint-triggered joint regulator investigation with formal findings
rows.summary: Canada's federal and three provincial privacy regulators jointly investigated ChatGPT and issued findings in 2026 on consent, transparency, accuracy, and data-handling — well-founded and conditionally resolved under PIPEDA, well-founded but unresolved for BC/AB.
rows.caveat: The 2026-05-06 findings date is the anchor; the joint investigation was launched 2023-05-25 (per a research pass).

rows.title: Joint investigation of Clearview AI (PIPEDA Findings #2021-001)
rows.status: Joint privacy investigation with formal findings
rows.summary: Canadian privacy regulators found Clearview AI's scraping of facial images unlawful and recommended it stop and delete Canadian data.
rows.caveat: Findings with recommendations; Clearview disputed the conclusions. Context only.

rows.title: OAIC determination on Clearview AI Inc.
rows.jurisdiction: Australia (Office of the Australian Information Commissioner)
rows.status: Privacy determination following joint investigation with UK ICO
rows.summary: OAIC determined (Privacy 2021 AICmr 54) that Clearview AI breached Australian privacy law by scraping facial images into a facial-recognition tool.
rows.caveat: Privacy determination context; not a court judgment.

rows.title: OAIC determination on Bunnings Group Limited
rows.jurisdiction: Australia (Office of the Australian Information Commissioner)
rows.summary: OAIC found Bunnings collected customers' biometric (facial) data without consent across 63 stores and ordered it to stop.
rows.caveat: Privacy determination context; Bunnings had a right to seek review.

rows.title: OAIC determination on Kmart Australia Limited
rows.jurisdiction: Australia (Office of the Australian Information Commissioner)
rows.summary: OAIC found Kmart's use of facial recognition to fight refund fraud (28 stores, 2020-2022) unlawfully collected biometric data.
rows.caveat: Privacy determination context; not a court judgment.

rows.title: ANPD preventive measure against Meta (generative-AI training)
rows.jurisdiction: Brazil (Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados)
rows.status: Preventive measure, reconsideration, then compliance-based modification
rows.summary: Brazil's ANPD ordered Meta to stop using Brazilians' data to train generative AI over fundamental-rights risk, then permitted a restricted resumption after approving a compliance plan (no under-18 data, 30-day notice, opt-out, ongoing monitoring).
rows.caveat: Latest officially verified ANPD position; restrictions could be amended by a later decision.
rows.sourceName: ANPD - Meta may resume AI training with restrictions

rows.title: Japan PPC warning to OpenAI on ChatGPT
rows.status: Administrative warning under APPI Article 147 (not a monetary sanction)
rows.summary: Japan's PPC formally warned OpenAI in June 2023 about ChatGPT's handling of sensitive personal information and transparency of processing purposes.
rows.caveat: Administrative warning, not an adjudicated sanction. Context only.

rows.title: ANI Media Pvt. Ltd. v. OpenAI OpCo LLC
rows.status: Court reserved judgment on interim application
rows.summary: India's flagship news-publisher AI copyright case: ANI's claim against OpenAI over use of its content; the Delhi High Court reserved judgment on the interim application in March 2026.
rows.caveat: No post-27-March-2026 dispositive order was verified; the Delhi High Court case-history portal is captcha-gated and could not be exhaustively checked.
rows.sourceName: High Court of Delhi - ANI v OpenAI order

rows.title: FTC action against Evolv Technologies (AI weapons-screening claims)
rows.jurisdiction: United States (Federal Trade Commission / D. Mass.)
rows.summary: FTC alleged Evolv Technologies deceptively advertised that its AI-powered weapons-detection scanners (used in 800+ schools) could detect all weapons; settled by a stipulated order restricting such claims.
rows.caveat: Consumer-protection enforcement over AI marketing claims; needs maintainer review against the FTC order PDF.

rows.title: FTC order against accessiBe ($1M, AI accessibility-widget claims)
rows.status: FTC complaint, proposed order, then final order
rows.summary: FTC required accessiBe to pay $1M to settle allegations it misrepresented that its AI accessibility widget could make websites WCAG-compliant, and that it used undisclosed paid reviews.
rows.caveat: Consumer-protection enforcement over AI marketing claims; needs maintainer review against the FTC order.

rows.title: Texas AG settlement with Pieces Technologies (healthcare generative AI)
rows.summary: The first U.S. state-attorney-general settlement over deceptive generative-AI marketing: Texas alleged Pieces' clinical-AI accuracy metrics were likely inaccurate and could mislead hospitals.
rows.caveat: State-AG consumer-protection settlement; needs maintainer review against the assurance of voluntary compliance.
rows.sourceName: Texas Attorney General - Pieces Technologies settlement

rows.title: CNIL sanction against Clearview AI (€20M, SAN-2022-019)
rows.summary: France's CNIL fined Clearview AI €20m (Deliberation SAN-2022-019) for scraping facial images and processing biometric data without a legal basis, later adding a €5.2m penalty payment for non-compliance.
rows.caveat: GDPR enforcement on biometric AI; needs maintainer review against the CNIL deliberation text.

rows.title: Garante fine against Luka Inc. (Replika chatbot), €5M
rows.jurisdiction: Italy (Garante per la protezione dei dati personali)
rows.summary: Italy's Garante fined Luka Inc., maker of the Replika companion chatbot, €5m for processing user data without a legal basis and failing to prevent minors' access.
rows.caveat: GDPR enforcement on a generative-AI companion app; needs maintainer review against the Garante decision.

rows.title: AEPD precautionary measure against Worldcoin / Tools for Humanity
rows.summary: Spain's AEPD ordered Worldcoin (Tools for Humanity) to stop collecting iris-biometric data in Spain over consent, minors, and consent-withdrawal concerns; a provisional GDPR measure upheld by the National Court.
rows.caveat: Provisional (3-month) GDPR measure, not a final sanction; needs maintainer review against the AEPD resolution.

coverageReport.caveat: Corpus coverage is an editorial snapshot. Missing records mean not yet collected, not absence of governance activity.
